page: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This server features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 12 physical cores and 24 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes an L3 cache of 260 MB, alongside L1d and L2 caches of 48 KB and 2 MB respectively. It is equipped with 1008.0 GB of system memory, yielding 42.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage consists of a 2835 GB disk, and the server is premium storage capable. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core processing, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks, both read and write at 256 MB, rank in the top 25%, indicating efficient data transfer for large datasets. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also rank in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, as measured by Geekbench, falls into the top 25%, while stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Passmark CPU scores remain in the average middle 50% range.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 42.0 GB per core positions this server as a memory-optimized resource. This high density of RAM relative to compute cores provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. However, the moderate multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks represents a tradeoff for compute-heavy parallel tasks. The hardware configuration and benchmark profile make this server suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, text processing, and caching systems such as Redis.
description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server from the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 12 physical cores, 24 vCPUs, and a 4.2 GHz clock speed, supported by a 260 MB L3 cache. The system is configured with 1008.0 GB of RAM, providing 42.0 GB per core, and includes 2835 GB of local storage. Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core and text processing performance, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression speeds. Multi-core and database benchmarks fall within the average range. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound applications, though multi-core compute scaling is moderate. This server is suited for memory-intensive databases, text processing, and caching workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX48-24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 1008 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive database workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-capacity RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and integrated local storage for memory-intensive database workloads.

1008.0 GB of RAM providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 42.0 GB per core
2835 GB of bundled local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing benchmark performance
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching, and text processing
